chosenConcept: मौन में सैलाब / Maun mein Sailab — "The Flood Within the Silence." A held-breath spine (Direction C) made physical by a single rising waterline (Direction A) and read as a sacred unrolled manuscript (Direction B). The whole document is one chest holding a breath: almost nothing moves, the negative space grieves, a dark waterline rises beneath everything as you scroll and is held — never spilling past the words — and opening a poem is the one permitted exhale, an unfurling folio that floods up from below. One ease, one rising waterline, one gold word per block, one exhale per poem.
rationale: Direction C ("Maun / Held Breath") is the spine because restraint IS the brief — "quiet, devastating, expensive" — and near-stillness that still feels alive is the hardest, most jury-screenshot-worthy thing to execute. But pure stillness risks reading as empty, so I graft Direction A's single load-bearing mechanic: a dark waterline that the reader RAISES by scrolling and that is HELD just short of the words — this makes the poem's thesis ("where words stop, floods are buried alive") a bodily interaction rather than a decoration, and it gives the otherwise-still page a slow accumulating dread. From Direction B I take the manuscript framing for the reading experience only (numbered folios № 001–003, the unfurl/roll-up open-close, the illuminated drop-cap, the optional opt-in paper-rustle), because turning a CMS modal into a ritual of being entrusted with a private manuscript is the emotional payoff that elevates the reading view. The three directions converge on the SAME motion verb (ink settling / surfacing through water on the existing [0.16,1,0.3,1] ease), the SAME Devanagari word-level law, and the SAME rationed gold — so this is one vision, not a mashup. I deliberately REJECT Direction B's site-wide ink-nib custom cursor as the default (it risks the "cheap gimmick" all three directions warn about and fights the stillness); instead the cursor is a near-invisible inertial ink-dot that only manifests over interactive elements, gated hard on pointer:fine. I reject Direction A's live feDisplacement ripple on cards (GPU sink, gimmick creep) in favor of the quieter card-breath from C. The result: stillness that breathes, a flood you raise and are not allowed to spill, and a manuscript you are slowly given permission to read.
narrativeSpine: One sustained inhale the reader is never quite allowed to release. ACT I — SURFACE / THE COVER (Hero): we open dry, composed, vast with empty paper. Masthead rule scores itself left-to-right like a ruled margin. The Devanagari headline does not slide — it SURFACES: each word rises from y+24 and blur(8px)→0 on the house ease, word-by-word, as if condensing out of wet paper; the gold word सैलाब (flood) clarifies LAST and a half-beat slower, the eye landing on "flood." The bua.png figure breathes (8s, scale+y+opacity micro-swell). A single faint dark waterline already rests low in the viewport — water present, contained. ACT II — PRESSURE (PullQuote): the chest at its tightest. As the band centers, its two hairline rules draw inward toward the label अनकहा दर्द, tightening the frame. The waterline, driven by global scroll, has risen to here and HOLDS — a soft dark wash quivering (±2px) just below the centered couplet, wanting to spill but not allowed. The single gold word छलकना ("to spill") gains an almost-subliminal 3px / 5s tremor — the held tear that never falls. This is the thesis rendered as motion-restraint. ACT III — THE INDEX (संग्रह): breath returned to the reader. Three poems as manuscript folios № 001–003, ruled, ordered, in cool even stillness with generous gutters — three closed doors, each card a quietly breathing presence (6px breath-settle, not a button). ACT IV — THE EXHALE (PoemScroll): selecting a folio is the ONE permitted exhale of the whole site. The held waterline finally lets go: a near-black wash floods UP from the viewport bottom, the manuscript sheet UNFURLS from its rolled top bar (scaleY transform-origin top), the title inks in, and reading becomes wading — stanzas surface line-by-line at true reading pace. दफ़्न सैलाब (the longest, the drowning poem) is paced slowest, its lines sinking in one at a time so the page almost runs out of breath. The closing signature नीलू शोरी does not fade — it SETTLES like sediment coming to rest after the flood. Closing rolls the folio respectfully back up; the waterline re-seals. Emotional arc: stillness → submersion → constriction → the private exhale → re-sealing. Grief witnessed, never performed.

globalMotionSystem.name: MotionProvider (+ motion.ts init module)
globalMotionSystem.purpose: Single source of truth for the entire motion language. Registers GSAP plugins client-side ONCE, registers the house CustomEase, wires Lenis to a SINGLE RAF shared with ScrollTrigger, and exposes reduced-motion + pointer-fine state via context so no component re-queries matchMedia. This is the foundation everything else depends on — build first.
globalMotionSystem.api: Wrap <body> children in <MotionProvider>. Internally: import {gsap} from 'gsap'; gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger, SplitText, CustomEase, Flip, Observer); CustomEase.create('breath','M0,0 C0.16,1 0.3,1 1,1') (mirrors existing cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)); also CustomEase 'sediment' for the slow settle. Refactor SmoothScroll.tsx to NOT run its own requestAnimationFrame; instead: gsap.ticker.add((t)=>lenis.raf(t*1000)); gsap.ticker.lagSmoothing(0); lenis.on('scroll', ScrollTrigger.update). Exposes useMotion() => { reduce:boolean, fine:boolean, lenis:Lenis, ease:'breath' }. SSR-safe: all GSAP inside useEffect; provider is 'use client'.
globalMotionSystem.implementationNotes: CRITICAL single-RAF: delete the local raf loop in SmoothScroll, hand lenis.raf to gsap.ticker — the #1 jank risk in all three directions. globals.css already warns against background-attachment:fixed; honor it. ScrollTrigger does NOT need scrollerProxy for the main document (Lenis transforms native scroll, window scroll stays truthful). The reading overlay is a SEPARATE scroll container (data-lenis-prevent) — see useStaggerReveal note for how that is handled without a second Lenis.

globalMotionSystem.name: useDevanagariReveal
globalMotionSystem.purpose: The Devanagari-SAFE word/line surfacing reveal — the site's signature 'surfacing through water' arrival, used on the Hero headline, PullQuote couplet, poem titles. Enforces the hard constraint (never char-split Devanagari) at the API level so no component can violate it.
globalMotionSystem.api: useDevanagariReveal(ref, { split?: 'words'|'lines' = 'words', stagger?: number = 0.11, goldLast?: boolean = false, trigger?: 'load'|'scroll' = 'scroll', y?: number = 24, blur?: number = 8 }). Internally uses useGSAP(()=>{...},{scope:ref}); SplitText with type forced to 'words' or 'lines' ONLY (a runtime guard throws if 'chars' is ever passed when the node contains /[ऀ-ॿ]/). gsap.from on the split units: { y, filter:`blur(${blur}px)`, opacity:0, ease:'breath', duration:1.0, stagger }. If goldLast, the element marked [data-gold] gets +0.25s delay and duration*1.3 plus a one-shot scale 1.015→1. Latin-only nodes may opt into char-split via a separate explicit flag.
globalMotionSystem.implementationNotes: SplitText.revert() in useGSAP cleanup to restore DOM (essential for the modal mount/unmount + Next fast-refresh). will-change:filter set by GSAP just-in-time, auto-cleared onComplete. matchMedia reduced branch: single 0.4s opacity tween, no split, no blur, no y. Replaces the framer 'reveal' variant on Hero h1 and the PullQuote blockquote so the whole site shares ONE arrival ease.

globalMotionSystem.name: useStaggerReveal (scroll-into-view, scroller-aware)
globalMotionSystem.purpose: Line-by-line / element stagger reveal on scroll-into-view for stanzas and section blocks — the 'wading, reading inks the page' feel. Decouples the main-document case (GSAP ScrollTrigger) from the overlay case (keep framer whileInView) so we NEVER fight a second scroller.
globalMotionSystem.api: useStaggerReveal(ref, { each?: number = 0.11, start?: string = 'top 85%', y?: number = 22, blur?: number = 6, scroller?: 'window'|'overlay' = 'window' }). For scroller:'window' → GSAP ScrollTrigger batch, once:true, ease 'breath'. For scroller:'overlay' → the hook returns framer-motion variants object instead (consumer spreads them) — because the reading overlay is its own data-lenis-prevent scroll container and adding a 2nd Lenis/scrollerProxy is gimmick-complexity we explicitly avoid (per Direction A/C tech notes). Per-poem pacing: accepts each=0.16 for दफ़्न सैलाब to make lines 'sink' slower.
globalMotionSystem.implementationNotes: Reusing the EXISTING framer whileInView line-stagger in PoemScroll (it already works, zero extra RAF) for the overlay path is the recommended, lowest-risk choice — the hook just standardizes the variants and re-tunes them to the house ease + per-poem stagger. Main-document blocks (संग्रह heading, footer, card grid) use the ScrollTrigger path. Reduced-motion: opacity-only 0.4s, content pre-revealed.

globalMotionSystem.name: Waterline (provider + component) — useScrollWaterline
globalMotionSystem.purpose: THE central load-bearing mechanic: one fixed, pre-rasterized blurred dark gradient layer behind <main> whose translateY is driven by global scroll progress — the buried flood the reader RAISES by scrolling, held just short of the words. Augments AuroraBackground rather than replacing it (blobs become deep currents below).
globalMotionSystem.api: <Waterline /> renders a position:fixed inset-0 z-0 layer holding a single pre-blurred radial/linear gradient wash anchored to the bottom. A module ScrollTrigger (scrub:true, integrated in the shared ticker) maps document scrollYProgress 0→1 to the wash translateY (e.g. from translateY(72%) at hero to translateY(28%) at the संग्रह grid) and a faint opacity 0.0→0.22. Exposes a CSS var --waterline-progress on :root that PullQuote and the Hero figure read. At the PullQuote a nested ScrollTrigger pins the wash crest just below the couplet and applies a ±2px yoyo 'quiver' (transform-only). On poem-open, a Flip/timeline lets the crest flood UP (translateY→ -10%) as the overlay enters, then re-seals on close.
globalMotionSystem.implementationNotes: Pre-rasterize the blur into the gradient image — do NOT live-blur a large element every frame (Direction A risk #4). Animate ONLY transform + opacity. Recolor strictly within palette (gray/gold #6f6f6f/near-black). The wash must NEVER cover poem text — clamp its max rise. Reduced-motion: waterline static at a low fixed position (or hidden), no quiver, no scrub. Drives the bua figure's bottom-fade opacity from the same var so on deep scroll she appears to sink into the rising water (opacity only).

globalMotionSystem.name: useBreath
globalMotionSystem.purpose: The perpetual 'alive, not animated' micro-motion shared by the bua figure, the breathing PoemCards, and the held-tear word — so every idle element breathes on ONE rhythm rather than each component inventing its own loop.
globalMotionSystem.api: useBreath(ref, { period?: number = 8, y?: number = -6, scale?: number = 1.015, opacity?: number = 0.04, mode?: 'breath'|'tremor' = 'breath' }). mode 'breath' → gsap.to yoyo repeat:-1 ease 'sine.inOut' on transform(y+scale)+opacity, large period. mode 'tremor' (for छलकना) → tiny y:-3 / period 5 / no scale, with optional blur(0→0.4px) at extremes, capped so it NEVER completes into a fall.
globalMotionSystem.implementationNotes: Amplitude discipline is the craft (Direction C risk #1): keep breath ≤9px/0.04 opacity, tremor ≤3px/0.4px blur. Transform+opacity only, sub-pixel tremor is effectively free. Paused entirely under reduced-motion (will-change removed). Replaces the ad-hoc framer figure-breath keyframes and the .poem-card hover so the grid reads as three breathing presences.

globalMotionSystem.name: useScrubParallax
globalMotionSystem.purpose: Lenis-driven opposing parallax (the 'page heavier than the cursor, moving through liquid' feel) for the Hero headline vs figure, the PullQuote band, and the in-scroll paper noise — standardized so depth is consistent and always transform-only.
globalMotionSystem.api: useScrubParallax(ref, { from: number, to: number, axis?: 'y' = 'y' }) returns nothing but attaches a scrubbed ScrollTrigger that tweens translateY from→to over the element's scroll range, ease none, integrated in the shared ticker. Hero headline gets {from:0,to:-90}, figure {from:0,to:70} (opposing, preserving the existing 'something pulled under' depth), PullQuote {from:60,to:-60}.
globalMotionSystem.implementationNotes: Replaces the current framer useScroll/useTransform parallax with the single shared ScrollTrigger ticker so there is no parallel scroll math. Transform-only, will-change:transform on active elements cleared when off-screen. Reduced-motion: from=to=0 (static).

globalMotionSystem.name: InkCursor
globalMotionSystem.purpose: A restrained, near-invisible inertial ink-dot cursor that only manifests over interactive elements — the 'patient reading hand' from Direction B, deliberately de-gimmicked to honor the stillness. NOT a glowing blob, NOT a default-visible nib.
globalMotionSystem.api: <InkCursor /> mounted once. Renders a small fixed SVG dot (near-black, ~7px), pointer-events:none, gated on (pointer:fine) AND not reduced-motion — otherwise native cursor only. Position via gsap.quickTo(x/y) for inertial lag (transform only). At rest opacity ~0; over [data-ink] elements (cards, links, close) it eases to opacity 1 and a faint ink-pool grows (scale, opacity only); native cursor stays visible underneath (we augment, never hide it).
globalMotionSystem.implementationNotes: Instantly disabled on touch and reduced-motion (Direction C/B both warn a janky custom cursor reads cheap). Transform/opacity only. If it ever feels like a gimmick in review, it is the first thing cut — it must be subtractable without harming the poem.

globalMotionSystem.name: FolioTransition (open/close) — useFolioUnfurl
globalMotionSystem.purpose: Turns the card→reading-view modal into the manuscript unfurl / roll-up ritual AND the site's one cathartic exhale (waterline flood-up). Replaces the plain opacity fade in Archive.tsx's AnimatePresence overlay.
globalMotionSystem.api: useFolioUnfurl({ overlayRef, sheetRef, topBarRef, bottomBarRef, originRect }). On open: timeline → backdrop opacity 0→1 (0.35s) → Waterline crest floods up → sheet UNFURLS from rolled top bar (transformOrigin:top, scaleY 0.86→1 + y settle + blur 6→0) → bottomBar trails +0.12s → title useDevanagariReveal fires. On close: reverse (sheet rolls back up toward top bar, then backdrop clears, waterline re-seals). originRect (clicked card bounding rect) optionally seeds the unfurl origin via Flip.
globalMotionSystem.implementationNotes: NEVER animate height/top — scaleY + transformOrigin only (no reflow). Drive via useGSAP synced to the open/close state; let framer handle ONLY backdrop opacity so the two libs animate different elements (never fight transforms). useGSAP cleanup on unmount is mandatory (overlay mounts/unmounts). Reduced-motion: straight opacity fade in/out, no scaleY, sheet static, waterline jumps.

globalMotionSystem.name: css utilities (globals.css additions)
globalMotionSystem.purpose: Shared static chrome the motion system layers on top of, keeping the B&W ink identity: the folio-number style, the manuscript vignette/deckle, the held-tear span hook, and a will-change discipline class.
globalMotionSystem.api: .folio-num (JetBrains Mono, № 001 style, wide tracking, ink-faint); .vignette (fixed inner shadow / deckled edge so the journal reads as a bound held object, pointer-events:none, z-0); .held-tear (the gold word wrapper — transform-origin center, isolation for sub-pixel tremor); .gold-pool (faint radial dark wash ::after for the sole gold word, opacity-animatable). All within existing tokens (var(--c-ink), --c-gold #6f6f6f, --c-paper #f5f5f4). Keep the existing static SVG paper-grain ::before untouched.
globalMotionSystem.implementationNotes: No new colors, no glow, no neon, no drop-shadows beyond existing restrained paper elevation. Vignette is a single fixed pre-rendered layer (no per-frame repaint). The .drop-cap rule already exists and is Devanagari-aware — reuse it for the illuminated initial of each poem's first stanza.

signatureMoments.name: The Surfacing Headline (gold सैलाब last)
signatureMoments.description: On load the giant Devanagari headline does not slide as a block — each WORD rises from y+24 with blur(8px)→0 on the house 'breath' ease, staggered 0.11s, condensing out of wet paper. The gold word सैलाब clarifies LAST, a half-beat slower (duration×1.3, +0.25s) with a one-shot scale 1.015→1 and a faint dark gold-pool wash behind it, so the eye lands on 'flood.' The italic English line fades up only after the Devanagari fully settles. Then everything locks to near-stillness; only the figure breathes.
signatureMoments.tech: useDevanagariReveal(headlineRef,{split:'words',goldLast:true,trigger:'load'}). SplitText type:'words' (matra-safe). Replaces the framer 'reveal' variant. transform+opacity+filter only.
signatureMoments.perfNotes: SplitText.revert() on cleanup. will-change:filter set just-in-time by GSAP, cleared onComplete. Reduced-motion: one 0.4s opacity fade, no split/blur.

signatureMoments.name: The Held Waterline (raise the flood, never spill)
signatureMoments.description: A single low dark soft-edged wash rests at the viewport bottom on load. As the user scrolls the whole document, its translateY RISES (driven by global scroll), tinting the lower page a cooler gray by the time you reach संग्रह. At the PullQuote it PEAKS and HOLDS: the crest quivers ±2px just below the centered couplet — water that wants to spill but is not allowed, the exact poem line — and it never covers the text. Scrolling IS the act of raising the buried flood.
signatureMoments.tech: One module ScrollTrigger scrub mapped to scrollYProgress driving translateY+opacity of a pre-rasterized blurred gradient layer; nested ScrollTrigger pins the crest at PullQuote with a useBreath tremor. AuroraBackground blobs reframed as deep currents (unchanged drift).
signatureMoments.perfNotes: Pre-blurred gradient image, NOT live-blur on a large node. Transform+opacity only, will-change:transform. Single shared RAF (no 2nd loop). Reduced-motion: static low position, no quiver/scrub.

signatureMoments.name: The Held Tear (छलकना trembles, never falls)
signatureMoments.description: As the band centers, the two hairline rules draw inward toward the अनकहा दर्द label (scaleX, transformOrigin center), tightening the frame like a held breath. The single gold word छलकना ('to spill') then gains an almost-subliminal vertical tremor — 3px, 5s sine.inOut, with blur peaking at 0.4px — a tear that swells but never falls. The one word meaning 'spill' is the one word in motion, and it never spills.
signatureMoments.tech: useDevanagariReveal on the couplet (words). Rules: gsap scaleX from 0 with ScrollTrigger toggle. Tremor: useBreath(wordRef,{mode:'tremor'}) on a .held-tear span. Lenis via shared ticker.
signatureMoments.perfNotes: Sub-pixel transform = effectively free. Tremor capped (≤3px / ≤0.4px blur) so it never completes into a fall. Reduced-motion: word static gold, no tremor, rules pre-drawn.

signatureMoments.name: The Unfurling Folio + Flood Exhale (card → reading view)
signatureMoments.description: Opening a poem is the site's ONE permitted exhale. The held waterline lets go and floods UP from the viewport bottom; the manuscript sheet UNFURLS from its rolled top bar (scaleY 0.86→1, transformOrigin top, +y settle, blur 6→0) while the bottom rolled bar trails down 0.12s later as if weight pulls it open; the title inks in via the surfacing reveal. Inside, reading is wading: stanzas surface line-by-line at reading pace, दफ़्न सैलाब slowest of all so its drowning lines sink in one at a time. The first stanza gets an illuminated Devanagari drop-cap. Closing rolls the folio respectfully back up and re-seals the waterline.
signatureMoments.tech: useFolioUnfurl (GSAP timeline + Flip from clicked card rect) for open/close; framer handles backdrop opacity only. Stanza reveals via useStaggerReveal(scroller:'overlay') returning framer whileInView variants (no 2nd Lenis). .drop-cap reused. Per-poem each (0.16 for दफ़्न सैलाब).
signatureMoments.perfNotes: scaleY/transformOrigin only — NEVER height/top (no reflow). useGSAP cleanup on unmount (overlay mounts/unmounts). Reduced-motion: plain opacity fade, sheet static, waterline jumps.

signatureMoments.name: Sediment Signature + Breathing Folios
signatureMoments.description: (a) The closing poet signature नीलू शोरी does not fade — it SETTLES, drifting from y−10 over 1.4s on the 'sediment' ease with a faint opacity rise, like silt coming to rest after the flood; the rule above it draws in first. (b) Each संग्रह card is a quietly breathing presence: instead of only lifting on hover, the plate carries a 6px breath-settle so the grid reads as three living folios (numbered № 001–003), not three buttons.
signatureMoments.tech: Signature: gsap.from(y:-10,opacity:0,duration:1.4,ease:'sediment',scrollTrigger:{once:true}) — overlay path uses framer equivalent. Card breath: useBreath(cardRef,{y:-6,period:8}); keep existing whileHover lift + .poem-card box-shadow transition.
signatureMoments.perfNotes: Transform+opacity only, zero layout. Breath amplitude ≤6px. Reduced-motion: signature plain fade, cards static (hover lift retained, no perpetual breath).

typography: Type is the protagonist; motion only ushers it in. KEEP the existing pairing — adding faces would dilute the discipline. Tiro Devanagari Hindi (400 only) is the sacred ink: every Devanagari headline and stanza at heroic scale (Hero ~132px, poem titles ~118px), line-height NEVER below 1.1 for headlines and 1.8 for stanza bodies so matras and deep conjuncts breathe and never collide — the air between lines IS the silence. Fraunces italic is the 'translator's hand': every English line set quieter, smaller, beneath/beside the Hindi, never competing; optical-size high, .display-tight (-0.015em) on Latin display ONLY. Cormorant Garamond italic carries longer body/standfirst at 1.7–1.8 line-height. JetBrains Mono is the manuscript apparatus ONLY — folio numbers (№ 001), dates, eyebrow labels — uppercase, 0.24em tracking; NEVER on Devanagari (the existing .label-deva guard is honored absolutely; Devanagari letter-spacing stays 0 always). SPLITTING LAW, enforced at the hook API: Devanagari splits at WORD or LINE level only (SplitText 'words'/'lines'), never 'chars' — a runtime guard throws otherwise. Latin may char-split but we keep it word/line-level for the slow dignified register. ILLUMINATION: the existing .drop-cap (Devanagari-aware, 3.4em, accent color) opens each poem's first stanza like an illuminated initial. GOLD (#6f6f6f) is rationed to exactly ONE word per major block (सैलाब in hero, छलकना in pullquote, the poet's name in footer) — scarcity is what makes it read as sacred rubrication, the site's only emphasis device and only chromatic event. Never animate letter-spacing (layout thrash) — fake any 'settling' with a 0.985→1 scale.
textureAndChrome: Keep and DEEPEN the existing identity — do not reinvent. Off-white paper #f5f5f4, near-black ink #161616, soft #565656, faint #8c8c8c, pure-black accent #000, neutral gold #6f6f6f. The static SVG fractal-noise paper-grain ::before (fixed, 0.3 opacity, pointer-events:none — GPU-cheap, no scroll repaint) stays UNTOUCHED: it is the dry fibre the ink wicks into. Atmosphere layers back-to-front: (1) Deep currents — the existing AuroraBackground blobs, recolored strictly within palette, drift durations lengthened toward 60–80s so they read as held atmosphere far below the surface, not movement. (2) The Waterline — one fixed pre-blurred dark gradient wash that rises with scroll (the held flood). (3) Paper grain (untouched). (4) A faint manuscript .vignette / deckled inner edge so the journal feels like a bound object held in the hands, not an edge-to-edge webpage (single fixed pre-rendered layer, no per-frame repaint). (5) Content. The bua.png figure is the soul of the negative-space thesis: keep mix-blend-multiply so she sits IN the paper, keep the 8s breath (now via useBreath with scale+y+opacity), strengthen her lower-edge dissolve, and drive that bottom-fade opacity from --waterline-progress so on deep scroll the rising water reaches her and she appears to sink/dissolve into the flood (opacity only). Add ONE restrained device from Direction C: a single faint #565656 hairline 'surface level' at section thresholds (under the masthead, framing the PullQuote) — the horizon beneath which floods are buried. The .ruled-soft lines inside the scroll are the lined paper grief is written on. NO new colors, NO glow, NO neon, NO vignette-as-drama, NO drop-shadows beyond the existing restrained paper elevation. Monochrome, humid, hushed, expensive — a sumi-e painting in a damp room that refuses to run.
cursor: A deliberately de-gimmicked InkCursor: the native cursor is NEVER hidden (we augment, not replace). On (pointer:fine) and non-reduced-motion only, a small near-black SVG ink-dot (~7px) follows with inertial lag via gsap.quickTo (transform only), sitting at ~0 opacity at rest. Over [data-ink] interactive elements (cards, links, close button) it eases to opacity 1 and a faint ink-pool grows (scale+opacity) — the 'patient reading hand,' restrained and monochrome, never a glowing blob, never a literal nib that could read cheap. Instantly disabled on touch and under reduced-motion (native cursor only). It is explicitly the first element cut in review if it ever feels like a gimmick — it must be subtractable without harming the poem. Rationale for keeping it minimal: all three directions warn a janky/showy custom cursor undoes the 'expensive' register, so it earns its place only as a near-subliminal augmentation, not a feature.
pageTransitions: Grid → reading view: the FolioTransition ritual (useFolioUnfurl). Backdrop opacity 0→1 (0.35s, framer) → Waterline crest floods UP from bottom → manuscript sheet UNFURLS from its rolled top bar (transformOrigin:top, scaleY 0.86→1 + y settle + blur 6→0), seeded from the clicked card's bounding rect via GSAP Flip → bottom rolled bar trails +0.12s as if weight pulls it open → title inks in via useDevanagariReveal → stanzas reveal on scroll (framer whileInView in the overlay's own scroll container, no 2nd Lenis). Close: reverse — sheet rolls back up toward the top bar, backdrop clears, waterline re-seals — so dismissing feels like respectfully re-rolling a document, not slamming a tab. Section transitions on the main document: each section block surfaces via useStaggerReveal (ScrollTrigger, window scroller) on the house ease; the संग्रह heading and footer draw their rules first, then content surfaces. NEVER animate height/top/width — scaleY + transformOrigin + translate only (no reflow). Reduced-motion: all transitions collapse to plain 0.35s opacity fades, sheet static, waterline jumps to position, content pre-revealed.
loaderIntro: A brief, reverent first-load sequence honoring the 'held breath' open — NOT a spinner, NOT a percentage. On a clean #f5f5f4 paper field with the grain already present: the masthead rule SCORES itself left-to-right (scaleX from 0, transformOrigin left, ~0.7s 'breath' ease) like a ruled margin being drawn; the पत्रिका wordmark and 'a bilingual journal of poems' fade up beneath it. Then the loader hands off directly into the Surfacing Headline (the words condense out of the paper one at a time, gold सैलाब last) — there is no separate 'enter' screen, the intro IS the hero arrival, so the first 1.5s sets the emotional contract: precious, quiet, surfacing from silence. The faint waterline is already resting low. Total controlled sequence ~1.8s; respects font loading (use next/font display:swap fallback metrics so no layout shift on the giant Devanagari). Reduced-motion: rule and wordmark appear via a single 0.4s opacity fade, headline static and fully visible, no surfacing stagger. Implementation: a small client gate in MotionProvider that holds the surfacing-reveal timeline until document fonts.ready resolves (with a ~600ms max-wait fallback) so the headline never surfaces into a font-swap reflow.
hardConstraints: 60fps / zero jank: animate ONLY transform + opacity + filter(blur, sparingly, always resolving to 0). NEVER animate layout/width/top/height — the folio unfurl uses scaleY+transformOrigin, never height.
SINGLE RAF: delete SmoothScroll's own requestAnimationFrame; hand lenis.raf to gsap.ticker (lagSmoothing(0)) and lenis.on('scroll',ScrollTrigger.update). Never run a second loop.
NEVER char-split Devanagari — SplitText 'words'/'lines' only; the reveal hook enforces this with a runtime guard. Latin may char-split but kept word/line-level.

Fully respect prefers-reduced-motion via gsap.matchMedia on EVERY animation: breath/scrub/tremor/waterline collapse to static, surfacing becomes a 0.4s opacity fade, content pre-revealed — true parity, not slowed.
Pre-rasterize all blurred atmosphere (waterline, blobs); NEVER live-blur large nodes per frame; blur confined to small text runs, always animated to 0; no perpetual SVG feTurbulence/feDisplacement.
will-change applied just-in-time by GSAP and cleared on complete / when off-screen; only on actively animating layers.
Keep the B&W ink palette and paper/grain identity exactly; gold (#6f6f6f) rationed to ONE word per block; no new colors, glow, neon, 3D, particles, or drop-shadows beyond existing paper elevation.
Bilingual integrity preserved; effects never cover or obscure poem text; the waterline always stops short of words; reveals always end fully sharp and legible.
useGSAP/gsap.context scoped cleanup in every animated component (revert SplitText) — mandatory for the modal mount/unmount and Next fast-refresh.

The reading overlay is its own data-lenis-prevent scroll container: keep its stanza reveals on framer whileInView (zero extra RAF); reserve GSAP ScrollTrigger for the main document only — no scrollerProxy/dual-Lenis complexity.

GSAP and framer-motion must never animate the same property on the same element (assign distinct elements/roles: framer = AnimatePresence backdrop opacity + progress hairline; GSAP = surfacing, waterline, breath, unfurl).
background-attachment:fixed stays banned (per globals.css warning); custom cursor instantly disabled on touch + reduced-motion.
